verdicts.note: PitchBook is a financial/private market data platform; 'Finance and Fintech' is appropriate.

dlthub evidence confirms REST API, API key auth in header format 'PB-Token {API_KEY}', manual provisioning via account manager, and endpoints for companies, deals, people, investors, funds. dev.writer.com confirms API key access requires contacting support/account.
dlthub evidence confirms 'PB-Token {API_KEY}' header format and that keys are issued by account manager. dev.writer.com confirms API key authentication and generation via support/account.

dlthub says 'Contact your PitchBook account manager to purchase API access/credits and request an API key'; dev.writer.com says 'contact PitchBook support for API access'. easyvc.ai confirms no free tier and enterprise custom pricing. No evidence of self-serve signup; must contact sales/account manager.
dlthub confirms credit-based usage and that keys are issued by account manager. easyvc.ai confirms no free tier. Evidence supports that access requires enterprise contract and manual provisioning.

dlthub lists base URL https://api.pitchbook.com and multiple endpoints: companies, companies/search, deals, deals/search, people, people/search, investors, funds, and account/credits. REST API v2 is confirmed. 'Medium' breadth is reasonable for this endpoint set.

The provided URL https://pathlit.mintlify.app/docs/nodes/pitchbook-agent is NOT in the evidence. The dev.writer.com evidence mentions 'MCP gateway overview' only as a general Writer feature; it does not describe a dedicated MCP server for PitchBook. No evidence of a PitchBook-specific MCP server exists; set exists='no'.
Evidence confirms API access requires a paid enterprise contract and manual provisioning by account manager. 'buildable-with-caveats' is appropriate.
Evidence confirms API access requires paid enterprise contract, manual provisioning, and contact with account manager/support. No self-serve key generation exists.
All three URLs are present in the EVIDENCE and their 'supports' descriptions accurately reflect the content.
0.72 is reasonable given clear evidence on auth and access model.
Evidence supports 'no-self-serve-signup' and 'credit-based-pricing'. The third flag correctly notes no official first-party MCP confirmed.

revised.name: PitchBook
revised.one_liner: PitchBook offers a REST API (companies, deals, people, investors, funds) authenticated via an API key issued by an account manager, but access is enterprise-only with no self-serve signup.

auth_methods.notes: Header format: Authorization: PB-Token {API_KEY}. Key issued by account manager, not self-generated via a dashboard signup flow.

revised.self_serve_notes: No free tier or self-serve API signup; must contact a PitchBook account manager, purchase a custom enterprise contract, and receive credits/API key. Each request consumes purchased credits.

api_surface.notes: Base URL https://api.pitchbook.com with endpoints for companies, companies/search, deals, deals/search, people, people/search, investors, funds, and account/credits.

revised.main_blocker: API access requires a paid enterprise contract and manual provisioning by a PitchBook account manager; no self-serve key generation, which limits automated onboarding for an agent toolkit.

evidence.supports: Describes REST API v2, base URL, endpoints, PB-Token auth header, credit-based usage, and manual key provisioning via account manager.
Confirms API key authentication and that access requires contacting PitchBook support/account for API key generation.
Indicates no free tier and enterprise-level custom pricing for PitchBook.
